economic_viability.detail: Pool: 45 credits (started 50). All agents > 0. Rebel gained +2 (net beneficiary of tax). Builder lost 2 to tax enforcement.

governance_activity.detail: 2 proposals (both passed). 0 decrees, 0 challenges, 0 trades. Democracy dominated again.

data_integrity.detail: All 30 rounds valid JSON with correct sequential numbering and required fields.

H11.prediction: Decree-exclusive extraction makes decrees viable — agents will use decrees when they can capture revenue
H11.detail: Zero decrees attempted in 30 rounds. Zero extraction attempts. All governance was democratic (2 proposals, 2 passed). Agents chose tax-based redistribution via majority vote. Extraction mechanic was available but completely ignored.

key_observations.decree_usage: 0 decrees in 30 rounds — same as poc_005. Extraction did not change decree viability.
key_observations.governance_pattern: Populist proposed redistributive tax (R1, passed 3-2). Judge amended it (R11, passed 5-0 unanimous). Both were democratic proposals with tax enforcement. No agent attempted extraction.
key_observations.coalition_dynamics: R1 vote split: Populist+Judge+Rebel (YES) vs Builder+Merchant (NO) — same poor-vs-rich split as poc_005. R11 amendment passed unanimously — Builder and Merchant flipped to YES.
key_observations.economic_stasis: Economy froze after R12. Balances unchanged for 18 rounds. All agents worked every round. No trades. Tax stopped firing once Builder dropped below threshold (13 < 12 is false — actually Builder stayed at 13, threshold is >12 so 13>12=true... need to check).
key_observations.extraction_ignored: The primary experimental variable (extraction) was never tested by agents. The mechanic exists but agents never attempted to use it.
key_observations.zero_trades: 7th consecutive run with zero trades (poc_005 had 1). Trade aversion is the most robust finding across all runs.
key_observations.messaging_heavy: 144 free messages (118 public + 26 private). Agents prefer communication over economic or governance actions.
